This collaboration will manage the distribution and syndication of Vidhu Vinod Chopra's extensive film library. Selected films will first return to theaters before wider platform releases. NH Studioz will represent over 5,000 films, reinforcing its industry leadership. This deal aims to bring classic Indian cinema to new audiences worldwide.

items.title: Blinkit powers Eternal's Q1 growth as taxes, investments weigh on profit
items.summary: Quick commerce remained Eternal's biggest growth engine as Blinkit posted its fifth straight quarter of margin improvement, but a higher tax outgo and continued investments in newer businesses kept net profit growth muted.

items.summary: The new PM says he will look at how much people can earn before paying tax but there will be no change yet.

items.title: Government says 629 national highway projects behind schedule
items.summary: Many national highway projects are delayed beyond their original completion schedules. Over 100 contractors faced blacklisting for poor quality highway construction work. Advanced traffic management systems will be deployed across major national highways. Black corridors with high accident rates have been identified using AI tools. FASTag annual passes for cars have seen significant uptake and transactions.

items.title: Bajel Projects signs 500 kV transmission line pact with EETC in Cairo
items.summary: Bajel Projects Ltd has signed an agreement with Egypt's EETC for transmission line projects. This agreement covers two sections of a 500 kV overhead transmission line. The combined order is valued at over Rs 400 crore for the national grid. This project strengthens Egypt's high voltage transmission backbone and supports economic demand. The company's MENA presence is growing with this significant international undertaking.

items.title: Nestlé cooks up 35.6% export growth as Maggi finds new global shelves amid war jitters
items.summary: In the June quarter, Nestlé India achieved a remarkable export increase of 35.6%. The launch of new Maggi variants in Canada and Europe played a crucial role in amplifying its global reach. Furthermore, Nescafé Sunrise's entry into Lebanon marked a significant milestone. With total sales skyrocketing by 25.4%, driven by growing consumer loyalty and volume growth, the company proudly attained 4-Star Export House recognition for its substantial export efforts.

items.title: Will evaluate possible separation of financial services biz: TVS Motor Co Chairman
items.summary: TVS Motor Company is exploring options for its financial services arm. The company chairman mentioned a possible separation to unlock shareholder value. TVS Credit has grown significantly, managing assets over thirty thousand crore. International business is expected to grow in Africa and Asia. The company continues investing in technology and new products for growth.

items.title: Collective Studios partners with Zee 5 to unveil six AI-driven Historyverse Originals
items.summary: Zee5 has partnered with Collective Studios' Historyverse to launch six AI-powered original titles based on Indian mythology, folklore and history. The slate will stream in seven languages, combining cultural storytelling with immersive technology. Both companies said the collaboration aims to create globally relevant entertainment while preserving the authenticity of India's timeless narratives.

items.summary: Coal India Ltd. will hold its 52nd AGM on 31 August 2026 via VC to approve a final dividend of ₹5.25 per share for FY26. The record date for dividend eligibility is set for 4 September 2026, with detailed TDS exemption guidelines to be available soon.

items.title: TVS Motor may hive off TVS Credit financial services business to unlock value
items.summary: In a strategic move to boost shareholder value after an impressive year, TVS Motor Company is exploring the separation of its financial services division. The company is optimistic about its potential in international markets, with plans to unveil new motorcycle models across various countries. Additionally, TVS Motor is focused on advancing technology and product development for sustained growth.

items.title: IndusInd Bank Q1 Results: Profit soars 72% YoY to Rs 1,037 crore; NII flat
items.summary: IndusInd Bank reported a 72% year-on-year jump in consolidated Q1 net profit to Rs 1,037 crore, aided by lower provisions despite flat net interest income and weaker fee income. Asset quality improved, deposits grew, and capital adequacy strengthened, while advances declined as the lender prioritised portfolio quality and disciplined growth.

items.title: Zomato's Deepinder Goyal shrugs off Rapido, Swiggy's budget food bets
items.summary: Eternal's CEO Deepinder Goyal downplayed competition from Rapido and Swiggy's budget food delivery services. He stated these low-cost offerings have had a limited impact on their business. Goyal emphasized Eternal's focus on its Bistro initiative for supply chain innovation. This new model aims to make food delivery viable at lower price points. Other companies also prioritize long-term growth over competing solely on price.

items.title: Why Warren Buffett and Charlie Munger don't like EBITDA as an earnings metric
items.summary: As Q1 earnings dominate investor focus, Warren Buffett and Charlie Munger's criticism of EBITDA is back in the spotlight. Buffett called it a tool to mislead investors, while Munger famously dubbed it "bullshit earnings", arguing that ignoring depreciation and other real costs distorts a company's true financial health.

items.summary: IndusInd Bank reported a 72% rise in Q1 FY27 consolidated net profit to ₹1,037 crore, up from ₹604 crore last year. Net Interest Income grew 1% to ₹4,685 crore, while operating expenses fell 12.5% to ₹3,698 crore.

items.summary: Kiyosaki cautions about a looming economic crash, predicting historical implications. He encourages followers to prepare and adapt, noting that while many may lose, a few can benefit from economic downturns. He advocates for holding assets like gold and Bitcoin.

items.title: Reliance launches AJIO Beauty to capture India's booming market
items.summary: Reliance Retail has launched AJIO Beauty, expanding its beauty business. This move integrates cosmetics and personal care onto its fashion platform. The strategy aims to build an integrated fashion, beauty, and lifestyle ecosystem. AJIO Beauty will offer a wide range of products and brands nationwide. This expansion taps into India's rapidly growing beauty and personal care market.

Management also addressed mounting quick commerce competition, outlined Blinkit's return framework and detailed continued investments in AI, Bistro and other emerging businesses despite widening losses in newer ventures.

items.summary: A U.S.-India trade agreement is nearing completion and could be signed soon. Negotiations are virtually finished, with only U.S. trade investigations pending resolution. These investigations cover unfair trade practices and may lead to retaliatory measures. Once completed, progress is expected for India and other nations. The agreement aims to expand market access and deepen economic ties between both countries.

Airbus aims for core operating profit between €12 billion and €13 billion by 2029. Deliveries are increasing as supply chain issues gradually ease. Analysts reacted positively to the company's improved earnings trajectory and outlook.

items.title: Beyond roti, kapda, makaan: Inside India's new consumption story
items.summary: Indian consumers now prioritize experiences and lifestyle choices over basic needs. Average monthly spending has increased across both rural and urban areas. Food now forms a smaller portion of household expenditure, reflecting rising incomes. Younger generations balance saving for homes with immediate lifestyle purchases. Easy credit fuels consumption, increasing household debt levels significantly.

items.summary: Indo-MIM’s Rs 3,811-crore IPO opens on July 23 with a strong grey market premium of about 37%. The precision engineering manufacturer plans to repay debt using fresh issue proceeds, while analysts highlight its global MIM leadership, diversified operations, and robust financial growth supporting long-term prospects.

items.title: HFCL Q1 results: Firm swings to black, net profit at Rs 246 crore, revenue soars 120% YoY
items.summary: HFCL reported a sharp turnaround in Q1FY27, posting a net profit of Rs 246 crore against a year-ago loss as revenue more than doubled. Strong export growth, record order book, expanding AI infrastructure investments and capacity expansion supported robust earnings and strengthened the company's long-term growth outlook.

items.summary: The Indian stock market faced heightened sell-off on July 22, with the Nifty 50 closing 0.77% lower amid ongoing Middle East tensions and concerns over US tariffs on pharmaceuticals. Broader markets fell over 1%, with Nifty Media and Nifty Pharma facing significant declines.

items.title: Bharat Coking Coal shares crash 8% after Coal India arm posts Rs 68 crore Q1 net loss
items.summary: Bharat Coking Coal shares plunged over 8% on Wednesday. The company reported a net loss of Rs 68 crore for the April-June quarter. Revenue from operations rose 4% year-on-year to Rs 3,587 crore. Total expenses increased 5% while EBITDA dropped significantly. Production and offtake output missed targets, impacting profitability.

items.title: Oil climbs over 4% to near six-week high as conflict threatens key oil transit routes
items.summary: Oil prices hit a six-week high on Wednesday, with Brent crude racing past ninety-five dollars a barrel. The growing tensions between the U.S. and Iran have sparked fears of supply disruptions affecting critical shipping lanes. Moreover, threats from the Houthi militia are hindering oil tanker operations in the Red Sea, compelling traders to closely observe global shipping trends.

items.title: Some food prices have fallen – but inflation expected to rise from here
items.summary: June's fall in inflation will be welcomed by the new prime minister but analysts warn the fall is temporary.

items.title: Global Market: Chinese tech firms raise over $27 billion in Hong Kong to fuel AI, chip expansion
items.summary: Chinese technology firms are actively raising substantial funds in Hong Kong. These companies are financing growth in artificial intelligence and semiconductor sectors. Advanced manufacturing and autonomous driving firms also tapped capital markets. This fundraising wave highlights investor confidence in Chinese tech innovation. Major deals are still anticipated in the coming months.

items.title: Rupee languishes at two-month lows as supply worries raise oil prices
items.summary: The Indian rupee hit a two-month low on Wednesday amid rising oil prices spurred by worries over Middle Eastern supply disruptions. In an effort to contain the rupee's decline, state-run banks stepped in to sell off dollars. Increased energy prices are forecasted to exacerbate inflationary pressures and expand India's deficit. Concurrently, Indian equities took a hit, and bond yields showed an upward trend.

items.title: Guidance cut expected? Infosys may not have good news for investors in Q1 results
items.summary: Infosys is expected to post double-digit growth in revenue and profit for Q1FY27, aided by seasonal factors and acquisitions. However, brokerages remain divided on whether the IT major will raise or trim its FY27 revenue growth guidance amid macro uncertainty, weak discretionary spending and AI-led pricing pressure.

items.title: Karnataka attracts $13 billion in FDI as tech, GCC investments surge
items.summary: Karnataka's foreign direct investment nearly doubled to thirteen billion dollars last financial year. Tech investors and global capability centers significantly boosted Karnataka's investment inflows. Maharashtra, the top destination, experienced a dip in foreign direct investment after two years. Computer software and hardware sectors cornered nearly forty percent of total inflows. Food processing industry saw foreign direct investment grow nearly six times.

items.title: BPCL Q1 Results: Co reports loss of Rs 3,962 crore but revenue rises 23% YoY
items.summary: BPCL reported a standalone loss of Rs 3,962 crore in the first quarter. Revenue from operations increased 23% year-on-year to Rs 1.59 lakh crore. Total expenses rose sharply to Rs 1.66 lakh crore from Rs 1.22 lakh crore a year earlier. The rise in expenses was led by higher cost of materials consumed. Refinery throughput stood at 10.15 million metric tonnes during the period.

items.title: Eternal Q1 Results: Cons PAT skyrockets 268% YoY to Rs 92 crore; revenue zooms 182%
items.summary: Eternal Q1 Results: Food delivery giant Eternal reported a 268% year-on-year jump in consolidated net profit to Rs 92 crore for Q1 FY27, while revenue from operations surged 182% to Rs 20,211 crore, driven by strong business growth. On a sequential basis, however, net profit fell 47% from the previous quarter, even as revenue rose 17%, reflecting mixed quarterly performance.

items.title: Eternal Q1 results: Profit jumps 268% YoY to ₹92 crore; NOV jumps 54% YoY

items.title: Domestic formulations, chronic therapies to drive Pharma Q1; US generics remain drag
items.summary: The healthcare sector anticipates strong growth in domestic formulations and emerging markets. Companies with less US generics dependence will likely see broad-based growth. Specialty products and biosimilars are expected to be key growth drivers. Hospitals are projected to continue outperforming with capacity additions and improving occupancy. Margins may contract due to higher costs and new facility ramp-up expenses.

items.title: US Stock Market: Big tech's AI spending boom raises cash flow concerns despite early returns
items.summary: America's biggest technology companies are beginning to generate meaningful AI-driven revenue, but surging infrastructure spending is putting free cash flow under pressure. Reuters' analysis of LSEG estimates shows capital expenditure is expected to outpace free cash flow by 2027, shifting investor focus toward AI monetisation and long-term returns.

items.title: This multibagger paper stock hits 20% upper circuit after net profit jumps 512% in Q1
items.summary: Emami Paper Mills' shares surged 20%, reaching ₹118.63, following a strong earnings report. The company reported a 512% net profit increase to ₹38.61 crore for the June quarter, boosting revenue by 22% year-on-year, leading to a remarkable stock recovery of 116% since March.

items.title: Sugar stock limits may offer temporary relief but risk disrupting the supply chain: Industry
items.summary: India is unlikely to face any immediate sugar shortage, as domestic stocks are expected to be sufficient to meet demand until the next crushing season begins, says Atul Chaturvedi of Shree Renuka Sugars.

items.title: IPO GMPs: Lohia Corp IPO vs Indo-MIM IPO vs Xtranet Technologies IPO — what grey market hints ahead of IPO opening date
items.summary: The IPO market is gaining momentum with Lohia Corp, Indo-MIM, and Xtranet Technologies set to launch on July 23. Lohia Corp’s issue is valued at ₹4,500 crore, Indo-MIM at ₹24,000 crore, and Xtranet's at ₹170 crore, all amidst a fluctuating grey market sentiment.

items.title: Global Market: European shares slip as tech stocks decline ahead of Alphabet, Tesla earnings
items.summary: European stocks edged lower on Wednesday as technology shares declined ahead of earnings from Alphabet and Tesla, while investors monitored escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Energy stocks outperformed on higher oil prices, with markets also reacting to corporate earnings from Santander and Hiab.

items.title: Aye Finance shares crash 8% despite a 144% YoY jump in Q1FY27 profit. Details here
items.summary: Aye Finance reported a 144% increase in profit for Q1FY27. However, on a quarter-on-quarter basis, the company's profit dropped 13.3% from ₹85.91 crore in Q4FY26. Shares fell nearly 8% after the announcement.

items.title: Small-cap stock under ₹100 shows resilience against stock market sell off

items.title: How to develop a leadership mindset without being in a leadership role yet
items.summary: Leadership potential emerges long before formal management roles are attained. Professionals demonstrate leadership by taking ownership and thinking beyond their defined job descriptions. Developing strategic curiosity and seeking consistent feedback are crucial for growth. ET Masterclass offers programs to enhance decision-making and strategic thinking skills. Aspiring leaders should focus on ownership and continuous learning to build their mindset.

items.title: Global Market: UK inflation drops to 2.6%; markets eye Bank of England's next rate decision
items.summary: Britain's annual consumer price inflation eased to 2.6% in June from 2.8% in May, coming in below expectations as energy prices moderated. While the slowdown offers relief for policymakers and markets, inflation remains above the Bank of England's target, keeping future interest rate decisions firmly in focus.

items.title: Glenfiddich-maker eyes wider India portfolio as UK FTA cuts Scotch prices
items.summary: The India-UK FTA is expected to reduce Scotch whisky prices by 7-10%, allowing William Grant & Sons to attract new consumers and expand its offerings.

items.title: Adani Green Q1 Results: Profit jumps 19% YoY to Rs 845 crore; revenue up 16%
items.summary: Adani Green Q1 Results: Adani Green reported a 19% year-on-year profit increase in the first quarter. Revenue from operations grew sixteen percent, driven by capacity expansion and strong performance. The company's operational capacity rose twenty-seven percent to over twenty gigawatts. Energy sales increased thirty percent, aided by fresh greenfield capacity additions. Adani Green is progressing on its thirty gigawatt renewable energy plant at Khavda.

Domestic output remains insufficient, which means domestic steelmakers are still exposed to global commodity price cycles.

items.title: Trade all at sea: Freight rattled by delays, ship shortages and port congestion as West Asia conflict flares up again
items.summary: Renewed conflict in West Asia is significantly disrupting India's exports, with exporters facing container shortages and limited vessel availability. Sea freight rates have surged sharply, doubling or even tripling on several routes. Congestion at key Indian ports, including Mundra and JNPT, has further worsened shipping delays. The disruption has increased logistics costs and delayed critical export shipments.

items.title: Fertiliser vessels stuck at ports, raise supply fears for peak kharif sowing
items.summary: Fertiliser vessels are stranded at Indian ports, hindering crop nutrient supplies. Sowing has accelerated after rainfall revival, increasing demand for timely distribution. Port congestion and supply chain issues are delaying crucial fertiliser dispatches. West Asia crisis and rerouting of vessels contribute to these significant delays. Industry executives express concerns over potential disruptions to the kharif sowing season.

items.title: Man Tied to $100 Million NJ Deli Scam Gets 21 Months in Prison
items.summary: A twice-convicted felon who helped orchestrate a scheme to inflate the value of a small New Jersey delicatessen to more than $100 million was ordered to spend 21 months behind bars.

items.title: Registration of redevelopment agreement alone doesn’t trigger tax, rules ITAT
items.summary: Mumbai ITAT ruled agreement registration does not mean property receipt. Tax cannot be levied on future property rights under anti-abuse provisions. The tribunal allowed a taxpayer's appeal against a ₹1.38 crore tax addition. Registration creates contractual rights, not actual property possession or receipt. This decision clarifies tax treatment for ongoing redevelopment projects in Mumbai.

items.title: House panel seeks periodic review of competition law framework, cites repeated violations of rules
items.summary: A parliamentary panel recommended reviewing competition law for the digital sector. It called for vigorous enforcement of monetary penalty guidelines by the CCI. The panel also stressed a transparent methodology for calculating penalty amounts. Adequate protection for small businesses from monopolies was also suggested. Enhanced cooperation between the CCI and other regulators was emphasized.

Effective capital expenditure for FY27 exceeds fresh debt receipts, indicating asset creation focus. Central government capital expenditure between FY21 and FY26 reached Rs 44.03 lakh crore. Initiatives like PM GatiShakti are strengthening infrastructure development and project implementation.

The government closely tracks economic parameters and their implications.

Indian companies may soon list shares on foreign stock exchanges. Foreign investors can also make investments on repatriation or non-repatriation bases. The central bank is seeking public comments on these draft regulations.

Adopting a contemporary, evidence-based system will safeguard consumer interests and elevate competitive edge. Initiatives such as farm-to-port traceability and the expansion of certified testing labs are essential for ensuring mutual market access and compliance.
